Patch Set 6:

Color me a bit confused, but wouldn't the point of the configure option be to link the tools statically (e.g. for use in initrd)?

Patch Set 6:

As long as you included the statically linked mount_utils_zfs.so and mount_utils_ldiskfs.so, then they could be used in initrd...

Patch Set 6:

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-11vm5 for review-zfs-part-1 received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/9652781c-dc19-11e5-b8c9-5254006e85c2). Ran 9 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 6:

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-16vm9 for review-ldiskfs received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/d851147e-dc1b-11e5-b40e-5254006e85c2). Ran 6 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 6:

The problem is that mount uses dlopen to load the mount plugins. It would require a large rewrite to avoid dlopen in the static case. Also we have to avoid collision in the namespace of each plugin.

Patch Set 6:

Andreas the other solution is to use ltdl which libtool provides to handle in a very portable way dlopen. That way the mount plugins could be built statically.

As a note Oleg reported his read only build/testing setup doesn't work with this patch.

parents.subject: LU-8275 tests: add flag to enable secret shared key for tests

commit.subject: LU-7356 build: handle configure options for mount plugin
commit.message:
LU-7356 build: handle configure options for mount plugin

Currently if you build lustre with configure options
--disable-shared and/or --disable-static it will fail
to build in the case of the mount plugins. The mount
plugins are always built to be sharable code using
libtool but this is not compatiable with the confiure
--disable-share option. The solution was to create a
temporary static library and for the static case we
directly link into the lustre utilites. If shared
libraries are enabled then that temporary library
is turned into a dynamic plugin. The lustre utilites
was updated to handle both cases.

Test-Parameters: trivial

Change-Id: I0eec25c8bce71c0e5c533ff3dfbe752677f37a26
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/17581
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
